It all began for me when I was about 12 years old and that's when my junk mail addiction began I was actually watching late night tv one night My my dad was watching the news and and I think you forgot that I that I was still awake And when the news was over all of a sudden these little infomercials came up and uh There was this guy on this infomercial talking about how he made money posting classified ads in in uh newspapers And I got so excited that I wanted to learn how to do it And so I wanted to I wanted my dad to buy the kit and my dad's like I'm not gonna buy that kit But I was sold the the guy sold me on on on the fact you can make money Using classified ads and so I went and I saved up 40 dollars first kid I had to mow lawns and do a bunch of work and I saved up money and I I bought this kit and the kit he started talking about how he was making money with classified ads He said basically he said I I took a classified ad I placed it in a newspaper And when I did I made $30 profit He said and then he said I kind of I was proud of that But I told my friends and my family and they kind of made fun of me And uh, but he said I took that same ad and I placed it in a thousand newspapers And then I was able to make $30,000 and I remember when he explained that like the light bulbs went off my head I realized that man if you understand marketing and how to create products and and do things like that you can literally scale A business really really big like I was thinking about people You know my my teachers I'm thinking about Other people like that who who go to work each day and they're working for you know 10 or 12 or 15 or 50 dollars or 100 dollars an hour or even more But no matter what if they want to make more money they got to work more hours And this was my big first epiphany that man if I create a product or a service and I sell it I can sell it all day long and it doesn't increase that the hours I have to work And uh, and that was kind of the beginning of my home marketing my home marketing journey And again, I was 12 years old at the time and so I started going out there and I started studying this and learning things I remember um, I bought a magazine called small business opportunities magazine and some of you guys have probably seen it before It's this it's literally a magazine that has 100 140 pages worth of ads and like two articles inside of it And uh, and I remember I was flipping through this reading all these ads and it just got me excited I started calling the 1 800 numbers in these ads and requesting their free kits And I started learning about about marketing and this is what that old offline method kind of looked like Okay, um somebody would see an ad like me in a magazine or a newspaper or they get a postcard or something Right and then they would call a phone number and then somebody answered the phone and they say hey You know thanks for calling for your free kit. What's your address? And they get the address and then that person would then mail them physically mail them a letter Um, and that letter they would open it up and what was it? It was a sales letter and that sales letter didn't sell some kind of product Okay, and this is where I learned back when I was 12 years old that this is how people were making money Okay, the guy in the infomercial was placing classified ads and doing this process I saw people in magazines doing I started getting direct mail I started just learning all these things and this is the way that they were making money There's some kind of direct response ads saying call this phone number or mail in You know a self-addressed stamped envelope to this thing and they would mail back a free report And the free report was just a sales letter. They would sell them some kind of product And remember I I I saw that over and over and over again and I got it. I said this is awesome But I didn't know how to do that. I didn't have any money to go buy ads. It was really expensive and and uh And so while I learned that I didn't have the ability to ever do it And I think that that's one thing that's that's unfortunate. A lot of people are never able to uh to uh to actually make money Back then because it just costs too much And that's the beauty of the internet a few years later When I wasn't 12 when I was closer to to 20 21 or 22 so about 10 years later I started learning about this whole internet thing and what was interesting is as I started learning about it It was very similar to the offline thing Except instead of having to buy an ad in a newspaper or a magazine or on tv or whatever We were buying ads on websites and somebody would click on that ad and it would go to a page It would say hey, do you want a free report? Give me your email address Okay, notice it's very similar to a real address, but give me your email address and I'll email you this report People give you the email address they click submit in the next page. What would show up a report? What was that report? It was a sales letter selling them something Okay, this looks familiar guys This process is happening offline is the exact same process that's happening online The difference is that for me to go place an ad It's a lot cheaper sometimes for me to send somebody a sales letter an email is free versus uh Direct mail cost a lot more money and suddenly when I learned this process I saw I said this is the same thing I was learning about when I was 12 But now it's on the internet now people like me can play okay, and this again I was 21 22 years old I was in college and I was learning this and it got me excited because I realized this is something that I could actually do I could do this and so I started getting into this whole business started Started doing this process I'd write ads and I'd send them to a page and I get them to join my list and I'd send them sales letters and sales videos to Sell them stuff and I started started Selling all sorts of things started growing my own company The first thing you got to figure out starts way over here to the left is who Who is the person you want to sell to who's your dream customer? Okay, now if you don't know who that person is right now, I promise you you're going to track the wrong person Okay, uh for years I was just selling stuff about how to how to make money on the internet And I didn't really identify who my target market was. I said, hey, anyone wants to make money on the internet That's who I'm selling to and guess what happened from that I got everyone who wanted to make money on the internet and a lot of those people the majority of those people Didn't have any money. They didn't have any time. They didn't have any skills any resources And they just weren't the kind of people who were going to have success Okay, and it wasn't until about two years ago. We we sat down and said look This is not our dream client. Okay. These are not kind of people we want to work with Are they who do we actually want to work with and we sat down we started thinking like who is that person? Who's our dream client and we started mapping out exactly who that person is and what they what they're interested in what they like And what their passions are and and and we started mapping out all these things And we really identified who are who our target customer was and I want to show you something kind of cool because Um The next two slides I think will be interesting to you guys because I'm curious that if these slides Represent who you are. This is what she's going to look like This is what she's going to be interested in and we we kind of wrote that out Then I took julie and I went to google and I went to google images I typed in julie successful businesswoman and all the all the different criteria of what I wanted and I clicked submit And all these pictures started showing up and I scrolled through it till I found my dream client So let me show how it worked. Probably not I think mike would I don't think either julie or mike would want that But if I created a product that was called dot com secrets labs 100 native our best split test winners And julie's already got a website and she's doing well, but she wants it to do better and she saw that bait Think she take it have a mike. Think you take that? Yeah, so I created bait that that my dream client would be attracted to Okay, it's just like I'm not a big fisher person But let's say you want to go out and you knew exactly the type of fish you wanted to catch Every fish has different bait right? Okay, so if you throw the wrong bait in you're going to get the wrong fish you throw the right bait in you're going to get Exactly who you want Okay, so after I've identified who my person is I gotta find out where those people are hanging out And then I create bait that's going to attract my dream client I throw that bait in there and now I can get my dream client. That's how I get them now after I've got them I gotta figure out where to want to take them Okay, too many of businesses online on offline and I'm sure too many of your guys businesses You're focusing so much on like what's the product I can sell what's a product? What's a product? But what you need to be focusing on is where can I take my dream my dream client? Okay, where can I take that person to serve him at the highest level? Because that's that's a business Okay, there's a big difference between a product and a business a product is something you sell and you make some money with A business is when you when you're working with somebody you're taking somebody from from where they are to somewhere else Okay, and uh, and this is kind of a big step. We're gonna talk about that. This is well throughout the the trading Okay, but that's a secret formula you guys Initially you need to think about that and focus on that. Okay, and you again you guys will see my process here as we go through. I'm practicing what I preach all day long All right, okay, so I was step number one step number two now Is what I call the value adder and this is I think when I first learned this concept and started understanding it This was one of the biggest ahas for me It helped me to really take our company to a to a whole different level almost overnight And it's a concept that that too many businesses don't understand and usually when I first start working people I want them to understand this piece of it because if you understand this piece Almost the rest almost all the rest of it will just kind of take care of itself. Okay, so this is called the value ladder now Again, the first time I started kind of learning about this I was at a seminar and I heard this guy named Mike Mike Littman talk and Mike was talking about his business model He said hey, I give away a free CD to get customers in and then from there I sell them my coaching I thought okay that makes sense and he said guess how much I how much money I pay to give away a free CD I was like, I don't know two bucks three bucks He said on average I'm paying between 30 and 60 dollars to give away Uh for every free CD that I give away And in my head I was like are you stupid? Why would you do that? You're gonna pay someone 60 dollars to give away a free CD like that makes no logical sense for me And I remember like the wheels in my head turning and then Mike got kind of quiet and he leaned into the mic And he said do you want to why I do that? And I was thinking again. This is me as a 21 year old kid. I said, you know, why would he do that? He said because amateurs focus on the front end Amateurs focus on the front end He said to to build a business the right way you have to understand You're building a business not just a product. Okay. I said because my business is structured the right way I can I can spend 30 or 40 or 50 dollars to give away a free CD and it makes sense because I'm going to make that money up on The back end. Okay. So amateurs focus on the front end in the inverse of that is people like us who understand business Understand that we're here to make money that we're professionals and we're trying to grow a company We understand that we focus on the back end. All right, so we hear that a lot. But what does that mean? This is Um a good example from a company. I'm sure you're all familiar with called McDonald's Okay, now McDonald's if you look at right now, they spend about a dollar 91 In advertising to get somebody to come through their the drive through window. Okay when they show up at that window The the average customer will pay two dollars and nine cents on a hamburger Okay, so that's something like a very good profit margin. They spent a dollar 91 They made two dollars. So so mcdonald's is making a whopping like What's that 20 cents 20 whatever that the math is on that okay 18 cents even even worse They're only making 18 cents for every single person they get through the drive through You're like man that ronald mcdonald is not that smart. He's a clown. He hasn't figured this out yet But ronald mcdonald is actually very smart, right? Okay, someone comes through he they spend the dollar 91 in ads They get someone come through to order a hamburger. They make their 18 cents. But then what do they say? Can you try upsell you fries and a coke you want to supersize that? I don't think I say supersize that anymore But if you look at mcdonald's growth when they added in the supersize upsell Now they said hey, do you want supersize this boom their company took off they exploded like never before And so they added fries and coke for dollar 77 more and guess what happened Okay from that dollar 77. They got to make and keep a dollar 32 profit Okay, eight times the profit by adding in the back end Okay, now a lot of your companies right now if you're struggling I'm guessing you're missing part of it. You're either missing a front end or a back end or something in between And so that's what we're going to talk about right now is the concept that we call the value ladder Okay, and this is what a value ladder kind of looks like. Okay, you notice on the left hand side We've got value And the the error goes up so so the more value you provide the higher it goes here and the bottom side we got price Okay, and so as we move this direction price gets more and more expensive now for me If I was if someone was coming to me and say rustle, what's the best thing you have to offer? It's always going to be back here at the top of the value ladder Okay, they're gonna receive the most amount of value possible and it's gonna be the most expensive thing possible Okay for us, we think the what do you think the most value I can possibly provide somebody is right now Okay, so for me it's a program that we actually launched recently and it was almost as a joke initially because I didn't know if anyone Was gonna buy it but we launched it and we recently signed up our very first customer and it's a million dollar package Okay, what the way it works? They pay us a hundred thousand dollars Me and my team my team and I I'm not sure we say that we fly out to their office And we set up their offer we set up a mini call center for them and we set up their traffic generation systems We drive traffic a hundred thousand dollars up front plus 10 percent of the profits up to 10 million dollars At that point we have made a million dollars and then the contract ends So it's a million dollar package. That's the best value I could ever provide somebody Okay, by far hands down. That's how I can serve my customers at the highest level Now if I was walking up to you on the street and I was like, hey great to meet you man I I I see you got a business here. You're chiropractor. It's awesome What I want to do is if you give me a million dollars, I'll take your business the next level you in If we think you're gonna say You're gonna say no rustling you crazy. I'm not gonna give you a million dollars You're there's no way you're that cool or even close to that cool, right? Okay, and then why is that it's because at that point I provided no value They don't know if I'm good or if I'm bad or anything So what we do in the business so we start down here at the bottom left hand Okay Well, we provide a little bit of value and a little bit of cost because because people will test something Right, they'll test the waters and see if they like something. They'll try it out. Okay This is and this is comes back to what we'll talk about here in a minute is our bait, right? But we're testing out some we're putting some bait out there in the water and someone's gonna take it Okay, for those of you guys who came through any of our funnels It might have been the dot-com secrets labs book might have been another product But there's there's some bait we put out there, right? And if you get that it's not gonna cost you very much But if you get that and you like it and you receive value what naturally happens next You're like man, that was awesome. I received value. It wasn't that expensive. What else do they have? And naturally if they receive value at this level, they're gonna naturally want to ascend up to the next level Okay, and here now you can enter into it something you sell them something else and they receive value at this level Guess what's gonna happen? They're gonna ascend up to the next level and the next level and the next level Okay, and that's how a value ladder works and all has to do with how much value you can provide somebody Okay, so for me I come in and we give away our free dot-com secrets labs book So many go through that experience They they get a bunch of stuff and they want the next thing and then the next thing the next thing and eventually we close people on our Our 8000 our coaching our 25,000 our our million dollar coaching and they move up through a value ladder Okay, and that's kind of how this thing works But it all has to do 100 with how much value you provide people Okay, if you're screwing people which happens so much on the internet Guess what you may get one one shot. You got one transaction might get somebody but that it'll end there But if you're providing real legitimate value people buy from you for me for years and years and years and years And that's the goal of building a value ladder the right way Okay, now on the bottom hand side here, you notice there's there's a dollar sign that's going across this way This is your continuity program because any good business you got to have a value ladder But you also need to have continuity where people are paying you monthly for for access to software for For something and we'll talk about how to how to create this later on in the presentation But just so you understand from this diagram what that is. All right Now I want to show you guys a value ladder for a dentist and I had an experience a couple years ago Excuse me that kind of uh illustrated this really really well. So when I first started my business I uh, we got started and uh, we didn't Um, it was just me at first and then my my wife and I and then we started hiring some employees And after we had four or five employees They all started asking us if we had benefits and I had never had a job before I didn't know what benefits meant Uh, turns out benefits means uh, you pay for people's insurance and their dental insurance all this stuff And we never done that before but I was like, you know what? Everyone's asking for it. So let's give everyone benefits. So we created this benefit package and so for the first time in like six years I had I had dental insurance, which was kind of exciting and a little while later. I got a postcard in the mail that said Come in from from a dentist that said come in for a free teeth cleaning. I was like sweet I got my insurance now. I got I got this teeth cleaning and and the bait was set, right? The company put out some bait and I took it and I called the company and said yes I want to get my teeth cleaned for free. They had me come in Came in and met the dentist met the assistants met everyone They sat me down. They cleaned my teeth for free and it was awesome I was super excited and while they're in there cleaning my teeth the dentist says hey, I just had a question for you Um, do you smoke or do you drink coffee or anything? I was like, what what are you talking about? So yeah, I noticed your your teeth are kind of yellow And I just I wasn't sure if it's because uh, you're a smoker or a drink coffee or what? I was like my teeth are yellow. Are you serious? He said, yeah, well, they they are but I'm like, no I don't drink or smoke or anything like that. He's like, oh, that's wow I feel kind of awkward now and I was like, well, what can you do about it? I don't want I don't want yellow teeth if you're noticing and I'm sure everyone else is too And he said well, don't worry. We have these uh, we have these whitening kits and we'll give you gel And we'll give you all this stuff to to whiten your teeth. I'm like, yes, please give me that And and like a good marketer did he provided more value because that he was able to sell me the next thing on his value ladder Excuse me, so then a few minutes later, he's going through and he said, hey, so did you ever have uh braces? I said, yeah, how can you tell? I said, well, your teeth are kind of crowding and that happens a lot of times when people had braces when they were young So my teeth are crowding. He said, yeah, but if you want we can create a retainer and the retainer will keep your teeth in place and that way, um, it won't look like you your teeth are all jacked up I'm like, oh, yeah, are you serious? Like my my teeth are messed up and uh, he said, well, if you want me to make your retainer I will I said, yes, please make me a retainer and um, and uh That's where the value ladder ended for me. I had my teeth that been really messed up I'm sure the next episode of said, hey, Russell, I'm sure you know, we can do this We can do and the next episode would have been cosmetic dentistry or whatever else it is Okay, and then when I was leaving my uh, my checkup that day, they the uh, the assistant said, hey We'll sign you up for six months from now to come back and boom. They signed me up for their for their continuity program Okay, so what just happened here? I came in a dentist put out some really good bait. It was a free teeth cleaning I came in and I received value my teeth were getting cleaned was awesome And then they figured out how could they provide more value to me and boom I I wanted to get my teeth widened and they provided more value and I kept ascending up this value ladder I naturally wanted to ascend because I received value at each point and I walked in on a free teeth cleaning And I left with a two or three or four thousand dollar bill. I don't remember what it was Um, and so that's how a value ladder works Okay, I'm sure you guys have all been to the dentist before you've seen this before But now you'll probably respect your dentist more when you see that man, they understand how to do this Okay and for me it was kind of sad because My wife went in two days later with her free postcard teeth cleaning and she walked away with whitening trays and a retainer as well So, uh, they got both of us and that postcard campaign probably made him eight or nine thousand bucks And I'll send done and then we've been clients now for them for almost 10 years. So it works you guys it works all day long All right, so here's the value ladder for my company just so I can be completely transparent You guys will see this and it'll uh, it'll it'll make a lot of sense Okay, so the front of my value ladder usually some kind of free quiz or a squeeze page or something Or I'm trying to get somebody in the front door after that we give away a free book free plus shipping Okay, I'm guessing most you guys on this webinar probably Uh got this book next we have our invisible funnel webinar Okay, for all you guys who are on this webinar right now. This is where you are in my value ladder Okay, you are currently on an invisible funnel style webinar where I'm training you for free and providing as much value as possible For two reasons first off. I want to provide as much value as possible second off If you like this, I'm hoping you're going to send up either my home study course one of my events or my inner circle There's my value ladder Okay, we do it all day long and then when you go through this process My goal is to get you on my continuity program, which is a software product called click funnels I mean sign up for that. I get paid every single month Okay, boom. There's full transparency. There is my dot com secrets company Okay, I can show you my value ladder for everyone in my companies And one of the first things we map out when we come into a company because the problem that most people have Is they come in they start a business and they've got one of these things. They don't have all of them They got one maybe two and they struggle a good example that is one of my buddies is a chiropractor And I'm sure I hope we got a lot of chiropractors on this webinar right now But typically most chiropractors they live and they die on their 40 or 50 adjustment Okay, so one of my friends is a chiropractor. He started this new clinic and um, and he started doing this He started getting clients and bringing them in and doing the adjustment and he'd make 40 or 50 bucks every time You get an adjustment boom and then the continuity for them is keep coming in a couple times a week for adjustments Okay, so he's got two pieces of this value ladder built out And one day he called me and said hey russell you're this great marketing guru Give me some magic so I can grow my grow my practice And we started talking a little bit and I was like well You know like tell me about your business and he explained it and I was like so all you do is just adjustments You think yeah, I'm like what else do you provide people? He's like that's it. That's what we do And I was like, huh, it's just that's kind of interesting. Let me think about it a little bit and I'll give back to you And so uh, literally like a week later Some of you guys know my background I was a wrestler in high school and in college and a couple years ago I started this wrestling club and I and I actually flew out the entire I had the entire olympic greco wrestling team moved to boise and we built a whole training center And it was it was really a really fun project for a couple years And we had all these guys training here for the Olympics And we had a chiropractor who's coming in a couple times a week to adjust the guys And one day after practice the chiropractor called and she wasn't able to show up But all the guys were like man our spines hurt our neck hurts, you know, I mean we're throwing each other around just Just in a pretty bad spot. And so um And so uh, they they needed to be to be adjusted and and one of the wrestlers was intelligent guy He said, you know what? I can figure this out and he went on youtube and he typed in chiropractic adjustment And within five minutes he watched someone explain how to do it He went into the room and he adjusted every single person on the team And I wasn't there that day, but he called me afterwards and told me said, hey I I'm a chiropractor. I adjusted everyone on the team and he told me the kind of the process of the story and I started laughing so hard I said, hey, I gotta call someone. I I'll call you back. So I called my buddy Who's who's an a chiropractor and I called him up and I was like, hey man, just so you know Uh, my friend and he knew the guy as well. I said he was able to to uh to replicate every single thing you do in your business And he learned it in five minutes on youtube And I kind of I kind of I kind of waited and what happened next was really funny my buddy the chiropractor He obviously got upset. Okay, he'd gone to who knows how many years of school to become a chiropractor and he came on He's like, he's like, you can't just do that. You can't He's like you're minimalizing what we do is a profession and this and this and I'm letting him kind of rant for a minute And I got a big smile on my face and then I stopped and I said wait I said if I was calling just to make fun of you, I probably wouldn't have called you But there's there's a lesson behind this I said What else did you learn while you were in medical school? Did you learn other stuff besides how to do an adjustment? Okay, we're gonna start talking about Where do we find and how do we communicate with our dream customers? Okay, you understand this stuff? This is where uh, I really think it starts becoming magical when you start understanding these pieces of it Okay, so we're gonna jump right in so the first thing I want you to understand Again, is there's three types of traffic Okay, it's something that I wish more people will talk about. Um, one day I was sitting there Excuse me, I was focusing on a presentation for some some of our um our inner circle members I was thinking about Just conceptually this whole business. I was trying to figure out how to make it simple How do I make it simple and all of a sudden I realized that there are three types of traffic And with that there are three types of websites. I said when you understand that it suddenly becomes really really easy Um to get traffic. So let me show you how this works. Okay, three types of traffic First type of traffic is traffic that you control Okay, the second type is traffic that you don't control And the third is traffic that you own Okay, I'm gonna say a couple things here. Um, that we're gonna kind of go over really really Really fast. I'm gonna come back over and go through it a couple times So it'll make perfect sense to you by the time we get here, okay? So the first thing what you understand before we Dive too much into that is you have to understand how how traffic on the internet kind of works. Okay, and this is what Um, when I when I first got this it's it's what made this whole internet marketing thing just makes sense to me. Okay um I started thinking about About just the fact that that people online they congregate together based on similar beliefs and values Okay, so when I think about um, the the word congregation, what do you typically think of when you guys hear the word congregation? Okay, for most of you guys, yeah, most of you are saying right now So you hear what hear the word congregation you think of a church, right? Churches they have congregations of people who who congregate together based on similar beliefs and similar values. Am I right? And so you have to understand that and so when we know that if We know that all of the all of the baptists are congregating together in one central location Okay, all of the the Mormons are congregating together all the Catholics are congregating together Okay, they're they're self congregating themselves together And after we know that if I was selling something to Mormons, where would I go? I'd go to the Mormon church because every single week all of them congregate together and they're hanging out And it's literally like like putting your fishing pole into water and pulling out who you want Okay, now i'm not telling you guys now or ever to sell things to churches But what i want to show you is when we talked about This earlier Talked about where this is the key guys when you understand this where this is if you look at traffic All right, okay, everyone's always figuring out how do I get traffic? How do I get traffic? And they they think it's like this magical thing, but traffic is not magical This is what happens online people they congregate together based on similar similar beliefs and values Okay, when I was wrestling and I wasn't and I was in college We had this mandatory study hall every single night because they I guess they assumed the wrestlers were done And that we weren't going to do our homework and it was actually very true But all of us had to do two hours of mandatory study hall every single night So we all go to study hall and there were computers and all of us would jump on the computers And every single one of us every night we'd get on one website. We'd go to the mat.com We'd sit there and we'd jump on the message boards and we talk about wrestling for two hours every single night And guess what? That's what's interesting. That was happening at every single college around the country and around the world And every single high school as well Everyone who is anyone who cared about wrestling they were all congregating themselves together on one central website Okay, now me as a marketer if I was going after wrestlers, where would I throw my my bait? Okay, I'd go to the mat.com. I'd be buying banner ads and buying email ads I'd be doing everything I can to fish people out of that pond because that's where they're congregating Okay, whatever business you're in right now. I promise you people are congregating just they're congregating And so for us we gotta figure out where are those people congregating? What websites are they on? What forms do they read? What on facebook? What groups are they in? What keywords they're searching for in google? What are the what are the where are those people out? What email? Newsletters they read what blogs are they on because if I Again because if I know exactly who my target market is and I can figure out exactly where they're where they're congregating Traffic becomes really easy you guys All I gotta do is I gotta find where they're at and throw my bait in the pond Buy a banner ad send an email out to the customer list. Okay, throw some bait out and boom I got him and I can take him or want him to go That make a sense you guys So that's that's the key to traffic. We understand that traffic becomes very Very easy. Okay. Now. Like I said, there's three types of traffic traffic you control traffic You don't control and traffic you own so traffic that you control This is the this is like I go like I don't own the traffic At google right google owns that so I don't own it but I can control it I can go to google I can sign up for an account and google let me pay them to be able to control that traffic I can say hey google here's a dollar for every single person that clicks on my ad when they click on it Send them over here Send me with facebook go to facebook. Hey facebook. You got a ton of people here Here's a dollar every time someone clicks put them over here to my website Okay, I can buy ads and people's email newsletters like my buy banner at same constant. I don't own that traffic Okay, google or facebook or the person who owns the email list or the the blog they own that traffic But I can come and I can control that by buying ads On those platforms Okay, that's traffic. I control now anytime there's traffic. I control. I always send traffic. I control some kind of squeeze page Okay, we'll talk about here in a minute, but all traffic. I control needs to go to a squeeze page Okay, second type of traffic is traffic that I don't control Okay, this is traffic that just shows up on my website randomly like someone on social media says something about me And all of a sudden people start coming to a website and I didn't control that they just show up Okay, or somebody goes into google to type in something weird and all and my website pops up on the top 10 results And they just show up. Okay. Good example. That is a couple years ago Um, somebody wrote an article about me and they said Russell Brunson is a little snot I took the article I posted on my blog and a couple days later one of my SEO guys came into the office laughing and giggling and said hey go to google and type in little snot And guess what at the time? I don't know if I still have not but if you typed in little snot into google Guess who was number one in google for a little snot? It was me Okay, now obviously that was not very targeted Uh, but what's interesting is that if somebody was to go to google and type in little snot And they saw I was ranked number one they clicked on that somehow they showed up on my website And I didn't control that person. They just randomly showed up. I don't know how they got there. They just did Okay, so anytime anything search engine related is traffic. I don't control It's anything like press Okay, if I get on tv or a blog or writes about me or something something like that I get a ton of people to my website tons of traffic, but I don't know how they got there. I don't control them I can't create that and you can do things to manipulate it But I don't I don't control that I don't control it just kind of happens So for traffic, I don't control I usually send them to my blog and on my blog You'll notice that I've got a goal as my goal is to get their email address Okay Now the third type of traffic is the best. This is traffic that I owned Okay, for me right now I have an email list of over a half a million people who have subscribed to my email list and I own that traffic Every day I wake up and I say, where should I send this traffic? I said I want to send it to this website over here I can write a little email I click send and boom instantly 500,000 people get a message from me and I'll get Thousands and thousands of clicks and that traffic goes to wherever I want because I own that traffic Yeah, that's the best kind of traffic that traffic is free the other kind costs money If traffic you control caught I got to pay google or facebook every time someone clicks on an ad Traffic, I don't control It just magically shows up you can pay people to SEO and write press releases and do stuff But it's not it's just not something I can rely on traffic. I own is the best So when I know that my goal as a marketer Someone who's driving traffic is to convert all traffic that I control and all traffic. I don't control into traffic that I own Okay, the way I do that is I drive these people through a little website that we call a squeeze page Become this page. They put their email address in they click submit and what happens That person's added to my email list boom now. It's traffic that I own. It's the best Okay, someone someone writes a blog posted about me. They go to dot com secrets dot com They show up on my page They they see at the very top if you notice any of my blogs the top one third my my entire goal is to get their name and their email address They cut my site they put an email address and boom all of a sudden guess what now I own that traffic Okay, guys because this is the best so I'm learning about traffic I got to find out where those people are at But I got I got to convert those people from traffic that I that I control and then I don't control into traffic that I own Because when I own that traffic Man, you guys that's when this whole internet marketing game becomes it becomes amazing Okay, I first learned that when I was getting started. I remember Um, that's probably over 10 years ago now. I was reading an article of this guy and he was talking about He said I'm sure you've seen online all these these these gurus talking about how they made 50 or 100 000 dollars in a day Or a million dollars in a day. He says you probably thought those things were scams, right? And me as a 21 and 22 year old kid. I was like, yeah, there's no way that that's not even the reality People I know make 20 000 dollars a year let alone in a day Okay, and so I kind of I was reading the article and totally just just caught me He said no the realities those things are true. You guys said this is how it works He says that a person a guru or whatever starts building an email list And let's say first they build a list of a thousand people then 10 000 then 100 000 then a million Okay, let's just say let's say you have a list of 100 000 people And you send an email out to that list of 100 000 people and you get just one percent of those people to open Okay, that's what 10 000 opens and you get one percent of people to buy That's Man of horrible math. What's that 100 100 buy 100 purchases and they're buying a 50 dollar product What do you just make you just made 50 or you just made $5 000 by sending an email to your list And that was in one day What if I send email to my list tomorrow? What if I get more people to open it? What if I sell for more than 50 bucks? What if I just do a webinar I sell something for 2000 or 5000? What if I sell a high-end coaching program for 20 000? Okay, they open up my eyes to the fact that now when I when I own traffic when I build my own list Everything everything becomes easy Okay, it's internet marketing if those who are struggling right now You will only be struggling until you have converted this traffic and traffic you own I literally feel like when we start new companies always like kind of pushing this this uh This bowler up a hill because we're pushing it we're creating the offer Drive and traffic we're getting some people but after a little bit all of a sudden I start building up my my own list and then it just takes off then that I own that traffic And literally for the rest of your life you will be grossly overpaid because you own that traffic I can send it anywhere I want I can make up an idea in the middle of the night Create a product really quick send down an email and boom make five ten twenty thousand dollars Okay, sounds too good to be true you guys, but it happens every single day And that's how it does by focusing on this on building up the traffic that you own Okay, so here's an example of what squeeze pages look like most you guys have probably seen it before It's just a page of somebody comes to and they give you their email address Okay, so any traffic that I control I always want to send them to a squeeze page because my goal initially is not to sell them anything My goal is to get them to give me their email address when I have their email address It's it's cash in the bank. Okay one of the metrics we we try to base our our business off of is we want to make one dollar Per month per name on my email address. Okay, it kind of stays similar to that When I when I had a list of about a hundred people I was making about a hundred bucks a month When I had a list of a thousand people I was making about a thousand bucks a month Well, this was about ten thousand people I was making about ten thousand dollars a month hit a hundred thousand I was a hundred thousand a month hit five hundred thousand and it keeps going on from there So those are some of the metrics you kind of look at as you're building your list Um to to know if you're if you're kind of on track if you're doing it, right? I want to show you guys how to reverse engineer um a funnel Okay, when I first got into this business one of my one of my early mentors was a guy named steven pierce and steven was uh Steven's a smart guy and I remember he told me one day he said he said you know what else? So people always ask me like how do you how do you create traffic? He said you don't have to create traffic traffic's already there You said figure out how to get your message out in front of it. So I started thinking about that. I said man, that's true So there's all this traffic already happening I got my message in front of it But who are the people that are already making money there like who's already in that river of traffic? Who's already in that pond making money? How are they doing it? I want to find out exactly what they're doing So if I can figure out what they're doing I can reverse engineer that and get as close to possible as close to what they're doing as possible. Okay, so um We started going through and started figuring this out So this is the the process we go through when we're trying to figure out where traffic's at and what people are doing that already works Okay, I promise you as if you do this exercise and I'm going to show you guys Um, you will be a thousand times closer than being successful than most people who get started Okay, so starts over here with with the where okay, where are your people at now? You're gonna find there's direct competitors And then there's indirect competitors Okay direct competitor somebody who's selling the exact same thing as you okay. I got a weight loss product Boom, there's a guy selling the weight loss product. It's a direct competitor Okay, indirect competitor is I got a weight loss product. There's the guy selling a juicing product Similar audience not a direct competitor. Maybe I can buy ads over there. So looking for these traffic streams That's why we design this look like little little streams, right? So my direct traffic stream and my indirect traffic stream Okay, so where are those things at? Next thing to find out is is these these five elements Okay, after I know where other people are at my my competitors are at what are they doing? Okay, who are the demographics? They're targeting. What's their offer? What's their landing page look like? Where are the traffic sources? What ad copy is working for them? Okay, I need to know what these five things are and after I know these five things are I'm gonna model what works I'm gonna find out what's working. I'm gonna model it. Okay The worst thing you could do is go into a new business and try to just to make something up from scratch Model what's already working. I learned this from Tony Robbins. He talked about like Everything he's wanted to do in his life. He's like I found the best person in the world I looked at him I model him and then I was successful very very quickly Okay, about coaching students who that's one of the main reasons that why they join our coaching rustly are successful You've proven it. I want to model you I'm gonna get in at the highest level and just figure out how to do exactly what you're doing Right, so they model it works. So let me show you guys how to find this out. Okay. This is really kind of a cool process So the first step is you find out who else is selling what you want to be selling There's a lot of ways to do that. You probably know who your competitors are already if you don't Um, one of my favorite sites to find this information out is a site called clickbank If you're a clickbank.com the clickbank is kind of like it's kind of like amazon kindle store except uh Their products don't go on the kindle but they before amazon came out. Okay, so that's kind of that's kind of how this works all right Next we're shifting to you guys now now we showed kind of Where where we can find our cuss our audience? The second part of the section is how do we communicate with that audience? Okay, and we communicate through a concept. I call the attractive character Now I initially learned this whole concept from one of my friends who's in the dating market whose name is john allinus And uh john teaches Men how to get women to approach them. Okay now. I don't know about you guys, but for most men That's like the the dream offer, right? I don't want to talk to girls. I'm scared of them want girls to come to me and that's what he teaches and that's what he kind of coaches on is Is that um that how to do that? So anyway, I heard him speak one time in a marketing seminar I thought he was awesome had him come speak at one of my events and in my event He started talking about this concept called the attractive character and I still remember like it was yesterday He was in front of the audience and he said look you guys are selling all sorts of stuff. I'm selling a bunch of different stuff he's like but what I found is that um, he said that uh That the law of attraction is the same whether you're trying to get people To like if you're if you're one of my guys on my list who are trying to get women to approach them Or if you're in business, you're trying to get customers to come to you The laws of attraction are the same He says that we start that process by creating an attractive character And remember we start talking about this. I had some some big a ha's and it just kind of opened my mind And um, this is kind of the the core concept behind the attractive character I'm going to break all the stuff down for you here in a minute um But but first I want to kind of just give you guys some ideas about how this attractive character works So good example. This is one of my buddies. His name is Jacob Hiller. And so he has made no Jacob. He is Literally one of my favorite people on earth. He's really cool. And so Jacob likes to jump Okay, that's one of his favorite things. He jumps like crazy and and uh, when he was in college He just liked it so much He started making videos and posting on youtube about how he liked jumping and teach people how he jumped and Things to increase your vertical and all sorts of stuff, right? And uh, he started making all his youtube videos and guess what happened He was so passionate excited about jumping He started getting this huge audience of people who were interested and they started following him and he started building up his platform You know, I don't know if Jacob was doing this intentionally. I don't think he was I think he was just passionate about a topic and he started just putting value out there in the world Okay, and for most of you guys depending where you are in your business. This is a great place to start Pick your passion to start putting it out there giving away giving away making videos or blogging or whatever platform You're the most comfortable putting it out there for him It was youtube and he's just jumping all these videos and people started loving him He started getting a thousand followers then 10,000 they kept growing from there and eventually all these people just loved him he built this attractive character and uh, and everyone loved what he was teaching and Somewhere along the line, I don't know if somebody told them or if he had the idea But he thought you know, I could probably sell these guys something that they love everything i'm selling Let me create a product so he created a product called the jump manual that teaches people how to increase their vertical And he went out to this audience that he already created this platform. He'd already built and he basically said hey guys Um, here's his ebook and now he sells uh for I don't know probably five or six years in romaine So over a thousand dollars a day consistently selling the jump manual Okay, pretty exciting. Don't you think you know, it's cool about uh, jakeb and his wife and his little family here is And their story is just awesome, but they have not had a home for like six years I think when they first launched this business they decided to travel and so they packed up all their stuff and they went to a country for three months and then they're making tons of money out there just doing their thing and And then they decided let's just go to another country and they they packed up their stuff went to another country and every three months or so they moved to a new country and they just live out of their suitcases and uh, they literally do that because they're The little website business and making a thousand bucks a day or more And and that's kind of what he does. So it's pretty exciting Definitely someone who who kind of figured out this whole lifestyle business we talked about right? He found out his dream customer for him It's basketball and football players. You want to learn how to jump higher? Uh, he found out where they're congregating right the basketball the football forums created some bait the jump manual And now he helps people uh to increase their vertical that's where he's taking them all And what's cool is you'll see him like he'll travel to kenya and they'll be there And he'll be out there making videos of him jumping with the masimora warriors Then he'll be in australia jumping with people and just he's taking this character And he's doing it all over the place and people love it. Okay, this is uh You know for years I've been sharing my attractive character with people and I'm not that good looking I apologize but I I find things in my life that that uh That people relate to and I share them when I first got started in this business It was my wife and I just got married and I was wrestling. We made a ton of money and right after me, uh, A lady somebody has nowhere ali brown. She spoke right after me. So here is the the worksheet that kind of goes with the attractive character Excuse me And so as you're starting to kind of design yours Here are all the pieces that are that are evolved. Okay First over here, we start With the elements the attractive character then the identities and then the storylines Now I literally can do it a full day long event just on this concept But obviously we don't have that much time. So I'm gonna go to kind of go through this fast But these are all the the keys of the attractive character. Okay So first key is every good attractive character has a backstory. Okay. What is your backstory? How did you get to where you are today right now? Okay, you've got to show that with your audience because the backstory is where Like let's say you're in the weight loss market If you used to be way overweight now you're skinny If you don't tell the backstory if you were always skinny and you tell people that They're like, well, you were always skinny. You don't get it. But if you say, hey, I was 300 pounds I struggled and then I came through this process. Now. I'm skinny. You're gonna attract people and say man This person's been where I've been I can do that as well back story is huge Second is parables. Okay. It's key that when you are talking to your audience, you are sharing parables Okay, I've shared a ton of parables with you guys already in the last hour and a half that we've been that we've been on Uh, when I when I teach this in uh, in a full like three or four day event I share a lot more parables and they're just stories to help people to understand a point, right? I share my fertility story now to illustrate a point I share my potato gun story when I'm trying to teach people about information marketing I share different stories different parables to get people Uh, to understand core concepts. I'm teaching and making very simple and easy to understand Okay, it's for all you guys you have to figure out what parables do you have In your life that you can share with your audience that gets them closer to you gets them More likely to buy from you Okay, number three is character flaws This one's gonna be very hard for most of you guys to share your character flaws But one of the best things you can do is share character flaws with people and they see that you have flaws Um, they're gonna be more likely to listen to you to respond to you and want to get closer to you Okay, character flaws are huge Um, you think about like the great superheroes in life. We'll get he-man superman batman Like what's interesting about them is not how perfect they are What's interesting is is the character flaws Okay, superman didn't have kryptonite. He wouldn't be interesting Okay, if batman didn't have his family and his loved ones that that could that uh, that kept him from doing everything You know that they kept him morally grounded. He wouldn't be that interesting the character flaws want to make people interesting So it's important to be sharing your character flaws. Okay, uh, and then number four here is polarity Um, the biggest problem a lot of people have with attractive characters that they're trying to be neutral I don't want to offend anybody and when you offend nobody You also don't excite anybody. Okay being neutral is the worst thing you could do online. You have to be polar. Okay, you have to have some polarity Um He thinks about someone like Howard Stern like he's built his business off of controversy And it's the group people who hate him who just despise him who don't like him at all But there's also group people who love him Okay, look at my business. There are people if you type my name into google There are people who do not like me. Okay, because I'm I have some polarity If I was appealing to everybody all the time, you know, it it I mean, it's boring nobody's gonna listen to you But as soon as you you have some polarity and you have some stamp you take stances on things and you push the envelope a little bit That's when you you'll see this this, uh, this polar opposite happened where a percentage people start not like you But a bigger percent will like you and see what like for you will start buying from you and and uh, That's really the key polarity is is huge. Okay, so those are the core elements of the attractive character Next over here we have the identity so each attractive character is going to have one identity Okay, um Let's see. So some of you guys and again one of these is going to fit for each of you Some of you guys are going to be the leader. Okay an example of the of uh, Of like a leader attractive characters like donald trump, right? I'm the leader. This is what we're doing. Okay So that's that's one option to become the reporter or the evangelist Like larry king and the number four and this is my favorite by far This is the one that I typically kind of take in my identity is the reluctant hero Okay, when I think the reluctant hero, I always think about froto baggins I think about the ring and he he got sent on this mission and he didn't want to do it That wasn't his goal, but he feels he feels drawn to that because Um, because it's his mission. It's what he needs to do. Okay, if you look at the storylines I share with people it's typically more of that thing like I don't I didn't sign up one day to become a marketing guru And to teach things to people but man, there were people getting screwed out there and people teaching bad information We built companies. We had success and and you know because of that I came out We started teaching stuff like and I reluctantly became this became the hero So reluctant hero is my personal favorite But for each of you guys your personality type is going to fit one of these You have to figure out which one that is and then embrace it and you can change throughout your your business and throughout your life Initially when I got started I was a reporter I was interviewing people like crazy because I didn't know anything and that's how I learned a lot of this stuff And after I had kind of done that then I shipped them more into like the reluctant hero or more the leader I'm just kind of shifting my identity. So you got to pick which one makes the most sense for you and then run with it Okay, and then as you're as the attractive character you're going to be communicating with your audience through story lines Okay, and uh, we're going to talk a lot more about story here in a little bit, but these are six core story lines we use a lot First is loss and redemption Oh, wait, let me see my notes here. I can reverse engineer that um, and uh Yeah, so okay So let's jump right here now Okay, so now we've got we talked about Where traffic's at we talked about reverse engineering where traffic's coming from So now we know that these are the sites that other people are buying ads on we buy our ads there Now we have traffic coming into our world now We're going to communicate to that traffic to that audience through the attractive character So you conceptually have the attractive character now we're going to put this into um into some framework, okay So somebody comes in they just joined my squeeze page. They changed from traffic that that uh That I purchased into traffic they own. Okay. This traffic came through a squeeze page now. They're on my list Now, what's the how do I introduce some of my attractive character? How am I building that relationship? How am I getting them to want to ascend up to my to my higher levels? Can we do that through the two processes? One's called a soap opera sequence and one's called a daily sign filled email Now I learned these two concepts. I learned soap opera sequence from a guy named andre chaperone I learned the daily sign filled emails from a guy named ben settle. What's cool is I went to one of their mastermind groups They're both there teaching their email marketing philosophies And they were sitting there arguing back and forth on who was right if the soap opera was right or if the sign felt Was right and I sat back listening to these guys and I kind of stopped and said you guys I don't think you understand how brilliant both of you are and if you use these two pieces in conjunction It can do some amazing things. I like doing the soap opera initially because when a new person comes into my list They're brand new. They don't know who I am yet. They just opted in for some freebie or something like that I'm taking them through his process now. I need to I need to warm them up to my attractive character I need to build this relationship as quick as possible And I do that through my soap opera sequence Now that I've got them through my soap opera sequence My goal is to send them up my value ladder and do that through my daily sign filled emails Okay, so let me break down how this kind of works First is the soap opera sequence. Okay. Now if you look at guys like andre chaperone, he literally writes soap opera sequences That are six months long. I don't know how he does it He's intense and he's got all these hooks and these angles and he calls it Soap opera sequences because literally what he does is just like a good soap opera, right? It opens all these loops in each episode that then close and then in You know in tomorrow's episode, but during tomorrow's episode opens up like 10 new loops Then close in the next day and so they run these things for like, I don't know soap operas and running probably for 40 years with the same storyline because they never they never let anything close, right? And that's why people watch these things every day because because how good they are opening and closing loops, right? So that's kind of how soap opera sequence works is is I'm gonna have these emails going out That are opening loops and getting people interested and I'm trying to hook them in but my my main goal I got two goals personally My first goal is I want to build a relationship with the attractive character Okay, and so for me, I do this through a five five day email sequence And you can make it longer you can make it shorter, but for me, this is how I do it five days And usually that that soap opera sequence is all It's building a relationship with the attractive character and pushing them back to my first offer the first thing I'm trying to sell them Okay, and then and then after that shifts the sign felt but we'll go through this first Okay, so soap opera so day number one when somebody first joins my list the first email I get kind of sets the stage Okay, so these emails typically saying hey, thanks so much. My name is Russell. Thank you for joining my list over the next Seven days. I'm gonna show you through my best strategies. Blah blah blah blah. It's gonna be awesome In fact tomorrow, I'm gonna and then I open my first loop tomorrow. I'm gonna show you blah blah blah blah blah blah blah Right so check my email tomorrow. We're gonna we'll dig deeper then Okay, so boom. There's email number one setting the stage for the relationship What's gonna be happening what they can expect and things like that Okay, now day number two My email is gonna start in a point of high drama Okay, if you look at good storytelling, I learned this initially from one of my friends and business partners dagan smith And dagan Spent like two years studying story and how you can use story to sell things And one thing he told me that was just I thought the coolest thing in the world. He said if you look at any good story He said the story always starts at a point of high drama He says the problem is that most of us when we tell stories We start at the beginning of a story. He said the problem at the beginning of the story is not exciting In the middle is where he gets really really exciting So he said what you got to do is you have to start a story at the point of high drama Okay, if you think about think about the good movies like, um Uh Like uh, well, I mean any of the good ones right cliffhangers one that pops in my head I remember I was like a 10 year old kid when that came out and I snuck over friends house and they were watching it I remember um, it was it was radar. So I wasn't supposed to watch it And uh, and my friend put it in the very first all I remember is like They're Sylvester Stallone and all these people they're they got this huge canyon and they're going across on this little zip wire They start going across and halfway through like someone's line breaks and they almost fall and they catch them They're trying to save them and all like this high drama and I remember sitting there and within like three minutes Look down my hands were soaking wet like I was just I got no any of the characters names I know anything all I knew is that it sucked me and I was at this point of high drama and I was like, oh And then it cut and then it goes back to the back story tells how they kind of got there Right and that's the best way to tell an uh a story through email as well day number two I start at the point of high drama. Okay, so my story I look at in my story I want to tell these guys to build the relationship with the attractive character What's the point of high drama and I share that first? And then I go to the back story the back story I go You know I kind of tell the back story which which goes until a point where I hit this wall Which is the frustration that I that I had Which is usually the frustration that they have okay if I know that they they're Trying to learn how to lose weight. I'm going to talk about high drama So high drama be like this spot where I got embarrassed about how how much overweight I was Until the back story about how I got there until I hit this wall where I couldn't progress It's probably the same wall you guys are hitting right now, right? But I've had this epiphany that changed everything and guess what I'm going to tell you that epiphany tomorrow Boom open the loop Open the loop right it's like it's so Barbara So day three now all of a sudden this person is anxiously waiting in my email because they have the exact same story that I had very similar And they want to know what my epiphany was because that epiphany changed everything that they want to know that is as well So day number three now I say hey guys. I told you yesterday. I was going to share through my epiphany This is what it was. I found out that when blah blah blah blah blah This is what happened it changed my life. It was awesome and that pushed people back Um, this is the concept we'll talk about when we get to the scripts a little bit more you guys towards the end of this Webinar but I whenever I'm trying to sell anything. I'm trying to focus on one thing like what's the one What's the one thing? Uh, what's the one most important thing I'm trying to sell here? So for these guys, it's if you weight loss could be you know The my weight loss secret or my tip or whatever the one thing is right the one thing that my sales video is about the one thing My webinar is about anyway. I'm gonna share this epiphany and how that relates back to the one thing the one thing that I did Was this okay? boom, so that's usually email number three email number four now I start sharing people the hidden benefits because people know like Let's say it's a we're getting a weight loss product. They know like if I lose weight I'm gonna be skinny and that'll be awesome. But I want to talk about hidden benefits like So day four is like, hey, I don't know if you guys realize this and I didn't realize when I first started doing this process But when I started losing weight, all these other cool things started happening. I had more energy people worked Trot treated me different. I had different girls approaching me and I started sharing all the hidden benefits of people Because these are the things when they hear these they're like, oh man, I want that too. I want that too Every little other product or service or thing they tried usually is very surface level. They share with you like here's the benefits of getting this Product you're gonna lose weight. You're gonna blah blah blah And if you can dig deeper on day four and kind of share the hidden benefits It takes them to a different level it helps them think start thinking Why yours is different? Why yours is better than any other solution out there? And the day number five is usually where we have some urgency like hey guys, this is the deal Let's offer the special thing whatever it's closing. I showed you how it's changed my life. Go do it right now Here's your call to action and boom So for me, that's kind of the sequence. I typically take somebody through on my During my soap opera as soon as they come into my my squeeze page this first little initial Sequence is that okay? Again, this is not set in stone. You can tweak things or move things or shift them to fit your story however you want The biggest thing is you got to be entertaining. You got to suck them from email to email to email to email um Each one for me I always and each of these emails always push back to the core offer So I'll talk about high hydra on the back string wall and like tomorrow I'm gonna talk about the epiphany but while you're waiting go check out this video right here And I'll push them back to the sales video Okay, so I I personally push at the end of each email pushing back to the sales video Because for me in most of my sales processes the traffic came they went through a squeeze page Then they saw my sales video they checked their email and boom they got email number one two three So they've already kind of seen this so i'm using these to push them back to my video At the same time as i'm building that relationship with the attractive character Okay, if you guys execute this part correctly the soap opera by time this thing is done They're gonna love you they're gonna trust you they're gonna want to buy everything else you've got Okay, that's the power of doing the soap opera the right way So the biggest thing is just be fun you guys all the attractive character stuff we talked about share those things bring them out And uh, that's how you're gonna get people to to really bond with you Okay, all right now after I've finished my initial soap opera sequence Then I want to like what do you email people now people always ask me like what do you do after that? so for me I learned this again from ben settle and uh, I start sending out daily sign for emails And all the daily like the reason why we call them sign filled emails um, do you guys remember the episode of of sign fell where uh, where they were starting the the tv show called jerry and uh, the producer mbc are like uh The producers mbc are like wait a minute. What's what's uh, what's the show going to be about like it's a show about nothing Like what do you mean like yeah shows about nothing? And the whole joke is just like it's a show about nothing which was funny because sign fell was a show about nothing Okay, daily sign filled emails are the exact same thing their emails about nothing Okay, you're just sharing some concepts some random thing with somebody and somehow tying it back to To your product or your service or whatever it is that you're you're trying to to send out somebody Okay, and so there's different types of sign fill. Well Actually, I just want me to share with you. I'm trying to find one real quick and share with you so you can get an example um Okay, this is Okay, this is one that I sent out so again this email had nothing to do with anything so especially like Like uh for internet marketers like you're probably like why in the world is russell talking about okay? So this is the email I sent out and this email by the way made me over a hundred thousand dollars So just kind of put in perspective of how well this stuff works Um and insert disclaimer this email was a freak Accident if you send this email out you will not make a million dollars or a hundred thousand dollars, but did for me So it's it's just kind of kind of crazy. So this is with the email subject line Jiu-jitsu is like wrestling for old fat guys and other marketing stuff. Okay. Do you guys never seen this email? So you guys Okay, is that random like who was Russell talking about jiu-jitsu is like wrestling for old fat guys and other marketing stuff So that was the subject line. Okay. Here's the body Uh, so tomorrow I'm fighting in a jiu-jitsu tournament for those of you on my list who don't know what jiu-jitsu is It's kind of like wrestling for old fat guys, which is great for me because while I still look like I'm 13 I'm actually getting a lot older now. I'm 34 years old and a lot fatter 30 pounds heavier now than when I was wrestling Anyway, I wanes in a few hours and as of right now, I'm still seven pounds overweight Good thing. I'm a wrestler and I have some awesome weight cutting skills In fact, I just found my old weight cutting clothes this morning. Check them out here. I showed a picture of them Then yes, they're a little bit tight But my three-year-old told me that I look like a ninja so it can't be that bad, right? Anyway, in about an hour I'm going to go to the wrestling room and within 30 to 40 minutes. I'll lose all seven pounds then tomorrow I'm going to get I'm going to Then tomorrow I get to step out on the mat with a bunch of younger faster Guys whose only goal in life is to choke me out or to break my arm whichever comes first I'm so excited. So why do I tell you this because this is the key you guys Notice I told a random story about nothing and the key is you have to transition to something something you're selling Okay, for me at the time I was selling people to my inner circle program So I'm now how do I transition this random wrestling story? Into into the product I'm going to sell Okay, and you notice like how many elements of my attractive character I've sprinkled in through that story right now you guys I told backstory. I've told I've told parable. I've told um I've told What's the one about? I just blanked out about Character flaws. I should character flaws there Like there's so many things I shared in that short period of time that bond me to people, right? Okay So here comes the transition So why do I tell you this because this week we did over six figures in sales Not this month this week and we did it without any product launches without any fillets And what's a pretty and while that's a pretty normal week for us this week was special because we also did it without me Actually being in the office. Haha Okay, so there was A perfect example of a daily sign for the email from that one email We got about 40 or 50 people who applied for inner circle and from that we closed over a hundred thousand dollars in sales Okay, an email about nothing just something that happened that day random So for you guys think about what happened in your day today Okay, think about the show sign felt right there in the coffee shop for a show boom that became an episode They were waiting in line at chinese restaurant boom that became an episode Okay, for you, what weird thing happened today that could become an episode in your daily emails Okay, you got thinking about something random cool exciting entertaining whatever that happened and how you relate that back to your Product or service that you're selling. Okay. All right, so here's for me how I structure mine. So there's episode style emails There's epiphany style emails and then there's educational style emails. Okay, so episode style is like what happened today Okay, that's kind of what this one was what happened today. I'm cutting weight What happened today?
