I'm not going to i'm gonna give you guys some tricks though Because this is where some of the more advanced stuff like after you start Communicating the audience like what are the other ways to to do cool stuff and so um This is a concept i call inception and i call inception because if you you guys all see the movie inception I hope if not that's your homework assignment go rent the movie and watch it But it's about these guys who uh who go into dreams and they try to place ideas into people's minds And when that person has the idea they think it's their idea and then they're more likely to act upon it, right? And so i started looking at our sales processes and and most people then they sell stuff They're so blunt and just like hey buy my stuff And i started looking at like if i can if i can change the way we sell where instead of me telling people They should buy my stuff if i can create a process and an environment and a language pattern That gets them to have this idea of man. I should buy Russell's stuff if i can cause that inception in somebody's mind It's a thousand times more powerful. Okay Um people they hate to be sold to but they love to buy stuff Okay, you have to understand that so i don't want to sell them stuff But i want i want to cause inception in their mind so that they are going to want to buy what i have to sell That makes sense you guys. I know it's kind of woo-woo at this point Uh, bow dig deeper to some of the concepts because there's a lot of stuff you can do and i wish we could spend a week on this right now But um, this will give you guys some some uh some gold nuggets at least So first thing to understand you guys is the attention span of a human being in 19 in the 1980s was 20 minutes Okay, 20 minutes. You think about like what interruptions you had okay once a day the mailman showed up Um, you know, maybe once or twice a week someone come knock on your door Uh, the phone ring maybe maybe two or three times a week Maybe once a day maybe twice a day if you were popular and that was the distractions that people had right what happens today Today's a little bit different right every three seconds. We're looking at our cell phones our facebook our text messages our twitter our emails like People are jumping from thing to thing to thing to thing to thing and literally in 20 minute period of time You've probably checked 80 different things. Okay. They did a study recently and the average Person right now today in our world guess how long their attention span is any guesses in the in the comments I would have I would assume maybe two or three minutes. That's kind of what I was thinking when I was reading this article about this The average attention span of a human being today in today's world is exactly nine seconds Okay Now when you understand that this is one of the keys this whole inception stuff. I'm talking about um, most people when uh Like when you start learning sales and marketing Uh, at least when I did I started studying for some of the greats right the people who have been teaching marketing for for 100 years And there are some amazing people that are that are top marketing some amazing books and you'll hear all the famous marketers They're like hey, you should go read Claude hopkins and gene schwarz and all these people's names and you should their stuff is awesome But you have to understand that they were selling in a time when the average attention span was 20 minutes Today we're selling to a world where the tensions the average attention span is nine seconds And if you don't understand that you don't understand the changes that have happened in our world today You're gonna get run over from people like me who understand that. Okay. So because of that we do everything different now We have to create our offers differently. We we communicate our messages differently You know all this edutainment stuff. I'm talking about like attractive character excitement storytelling fun That stuff is is necessary today because if you are bored, you're gone Okay, people today don't watch the presidential debates the most important thing in the world. Why because it's boring They want to go watch something on youtube that's you know, they're gonna capture their attention and keep it for five seconds Okay, that's the world we're in right now And if you're not if you're not capturing some's attention in that's tiny period of time you've got you've lost them Okay, and so it's key now There's a lot of different elements this whole this whole Inception stuff, but one of the key parts of it This is from a book from Blair Warren. I've never met the skype. I really want to Um, this is a kindle book you can get for like three bucks. It's called the the one sentence persuasion course And it's 27 words to make the world do your bidding And when I read this I was just like that dude gets it. He understands it perfectly So this is the one sentence persuasion course It says people will do anything for those who encourage their dreams Justify that justify their failures Alive their fears Confirm their suspicions and help them throw rocks at their enemies Okay, there's a lot of power in that Okay Look at the emails that you said are the nice and I look at the sales videos the sales letters the webinars all those type of things And see if you're hitting on these things Okay, people do anything for those who encourage their dreams. Are you encouraging your audience's dreams? Number two is justify their failures. Are you justifying their failures? Okay, yeah, you need to be It's not your fault You know, it's the big the big bad gurus. It's the food industry. It's whatever is keeping them back Okay, lie their fears confirm their suspicions And helping to throw rocks at their enemies So every time I'm writing copy or creating a webinar or whatever I always look at this and like how can I weave these things into to my communication? This is one of the big core parts about inception Okay All right, here's the the graph that goes with inception secrets So the top there is kind of that the the one sentence persuasion guide, right? Encourage their dreams justify their failures lie their fears confirm their suspicions and throw rocks at their enemies Now when I'm creating any kind of sales message Um, the first thing I'm looking at is every single person has like this primary question Okay, when it comes to your business now I first learned this from Tony Robbins and he he shared a concept It was date with destiny program that he called the primary question He said that every single human has a primary question That's always going on in their head over and over and over again It drives every single choice you make in life Some people's primary question is how can I do this better? Okay, and you know those people because every single situation they're in their life trying to figure out how to do it better How can I run faster? How can I make this business grow? How can I do this? How can I do this? And they had this primary question that's driving every single one of their choices? Okay, other people's primary question is why do I do everything wrong? Okay, that person drives their life. It makes decisions a lot different, right? Oh, why do I do everything wrong? Why do I do everything wrong? Why am I so dumb? Why can't you know, whatever that thing so everyone's got this primary question Now we can go off on a whole tangent on that. I'm not going to but I want to understand that primary question is key And it would be good for you to know your your core audience what their primary question is But more specifically when it relates to your product What is I believe this this also happens like a product or service level that people have this this this primary question and so Five or six years ago when we had we used to have a big huge call center were 60 sales people and we were selling Beginner internet marketers how to start a business online? And I remember one day I went down to the down there I was listening to these phone calls and it's like said 60 full-time sales guys and I would say Every probably 10 or 15 minutes. I would hear this this question come up Where the people would people would say hey if Russell stuff is so good Why don't you just give it to me for free and I'll pay him back from the from the money I make I used to think oh, it's dumb. Why would they say that? But they kept coming up and kept coming up and kept coming up over and over and over again And one day I had this epiphany. I said, you know what that's their primary question That's that's my audience's primary question. So how do I address that? How do I how do I help them understand that? And so um We I wrote a sales letter about that time for a product called dot com secrets x and in that in that video The I addressed that primary question. I said look all the other gurus out there keep talking about how good their products are and And uh, and if they really are that good, then why don't they just give you that product for free? And then if you make money, then they'll take your money Okay, and I I brought that question up and I said, you know it makes sense to me Does it make sense to you guys and the people that were listening were like, yes, of course Like that's what I'm thinking. That's the primary question that's been happening in my head I said look, I'm gonna be different than other gurus and I'm gonna give it to you for free You can come in here try out for 30 days for free and if it works for you, you can pay me if not then I don't want I don't deserve if I can't make you money I don't deserve your money and we flipped it around like that and I I created my offer to match their primary question And uh, we literally went from zero to 8,000 members in that membership site in a month Okay, because I hit the right primary question Okay, so think about that with your audience like what's the question they have and if I can communicate that very very quickly in the Exact same words that they're saying it in um, I can get them to to move and do a lot of cool stuff Okay, now the primary question leads to some kind of epiphany, right the epiphany that I had like I had this epiphany that That boom you needed this you needed the this uh this change And so I'm usually driving my primary question to some kind of epiphany And I'll show this a lot deeper in the sales scripts you guys here in a minute But just understand that the primary question is leaving some kind of epiphany Which then leads to a magic bowl and that magic bowl does your offer Right, um, this is that this is that that thing you have that's going to solve all their problems Back in the day we used to sell a lot more. It's like here's my thing And here's a million bullet points about why this thing is the greatest thing in the world Okay, and about six months ago. I had this I was working on a project And we had we had a sales video that just wasn't converting and we were selling a product had six hours worth of event stuff Right it was six It was two three-day events and all the videos and it's like literally one of my favorite products I've ever created and I was selling it and in there I was saying sharing all like you get this and you get this And I was trying to share all the stuff that they got because I wanted them to know every single thing that that was possible in this uh in this program and uh And it just I we we launched it twice and both times it did not convert and I was just like so frustrated I'm like nice people don't understand what's in here. This is like gold and one night I had saw how I said What if what if I just find the best thing that I sell and just Only focus on that one thing like we cut out everything else and just focus on one thing the one thing And so I went through my entire six hours and I found one thing that was most interesting And I rewrote the entire sales message just to focus on that one thing the one magic bullet and literally in in the six day event I probably talked about that one thing for 30 minutes But that's all I talked about during my sales message is that one thing the one thing the one thing I kept going on going on Going on it and the end I'm like, oh, yeah, you get a bunch of other stuff as well But this one thing is worth the price of admission and more And by changing it to be this this one thing It's one focus one singular focus conversions went through the roof and so for most of you guys Like how how can you take your offer and really boil it down to like the one thing? What's the one thing that they really need if you can identify that and make it a tangible Where they can see it and be like man, I need that thing that one thing Um, it'll it'll do it'll do magic Okay, all right now a couple other things and again, I wish I had days to go into this and I don't um I'm going to go into really quickly one of them is copy speed and uh and typically Um people write to people always writes. I wish I would have an example. I don't have an example here to show you guys right now but typically people would write sales letters and uh And when they do they I mean you've seen a sales letter there they're usually long blocky big paragraphs and things like that and uh a while ago we were doing some tests and I I was promoting some offers and testing them out and and there was this one offer that Um just converted better than anything I'd ever seen before before or since like it just did crazy stuff and And we promoted and I went back and looked at it later and I realized it was just it was written differently was written Um in this weird like high-paced fast, uh process in fact, I wonder if I Am I having example here? Some of my notes I was looking through today Yeah, here it is. So I was looking at this offer and I noticed that That uh the way it was written was everything was very short sentences with dot dots afterwards And there was never a sentence was more than like one line And I thought that's really interesting And so I I I met the guy who who wrote this way and I tested some ads That he had written and every single one was just the best converting ads that I've ever done I thought this is interesting. So I wanted to kind of learn that style and to figure it out. So Um, and that's why I call this copy speed because this this the way I write I used to take me I don't know almost two weeks to write a sales letter when I learned this process I was able to write an entire sales letter in about two hours. It would normally take me two weeks And they convert 10 times better Okay, so this is an example of the dot com secrets x intro because see how I written it see knows how short the Senses are they're all like incomplete sentences that pull you from thing to thing to thing and it's just it's very fast to read And it sucks you in so I'm gonna read it to you It says I've got a quick question for you. Have you ever wanted to work from home or own your own business? My guess is this you saw someone online promising you riches untold But there's a catch doesn't seem like there's always a catch yet to pay hundreds or thousands of dollars to get started Right before you make any money Don't they know that's why you're here because you need money Doesn't seem like that if all those gurus courses and software work so good and they really believe in them Why don't they just give you their money making system and then after you've made money then you'd get paid Okay, you guys see that So copy speed very fast speed and you see how I transitioned all the way to boom. Are you ready for the same change that's about to happen to you? I'm getting them say yes. Yes Um If you guys had a chance to read the dot com secrets labs book I talked about how we had an automated webinar that was averaging like nine dollars and sixty cents for every single person who registered And I learned this concept about trial closes and so I went back and I I like literally this is in in uh In my webinar a minute oh five I went back and I recorded these three trial closes and had my brother who does my video splice them in Then a minute 21 I dropped these three trial closes and then I dropped these ones in it I literally spliced those things into my presentation just asking questions to get somebody to say yes in their head and just by adding those trial closes we raised our uh, Our earnings per attendee from nine dollars and sixty cents like fourteen dollars and sixty cents something crazy like that The exact numbers in the dot com secrets labs book along with all the trial closes in there But it was crazy trial closes work awesome and then desire amplifiers Um, there's a ton of desire amplifiers you guys things that just get people to desire your product more And so when you're writing your copy and you're creating your things these are just things to think about Okay, again inception secrets. There's thousands not thousands There's dozens of different things I could talk about that we don't have time today But these are the core most important ones increasing the copy speed dropping trial closes Uh focusing on a primary question focusing on your magic bolt focusing on one thing What's the one core thing that you need to get somebody to understand to buy your product? Okay, I was talking to peri belcher, uh, who's one of the best online salespeople of all time And he was telling me that every time they have a video sales letter or something that is trying to get somebody to believe Two concepts or three concepts it fails And since they're trying to get them to believe one thing just one core message It always is a winner. So hope that gives you guys a little bit excited about just some of the the cool marketing ninja voodoo stuff you can add into your your sales messages All right, you guys let's jump into section number three. So the first section we talked about if you remember we talked about About designing your dream business Second one is getting your your dream clients and then how to communicate to them through your through your attractive character And now we're going to be focusing on funneleology, which is the study of funnels like these sales and marketing funnels You probably heard people drop those terms before but what are they? How do they work? And so I literally spent the last year So just going through all the sales funds we've ever done and really dissecting them and breaking them apart and seeing What worked and what didn't work and why they worked and and how they all work and from there um I really learned a lot of cool stuff and I wanted to share with you because I think there are things that um Are going to be a ton of value for you So that's what the funneleology section is the first thing we'll talk about are the seven phases of a funnel Okay, and I'm guessing that four or five of these phases you've probably never even thought about but it's all it all Ties back to we talked about earlier with the value add like all these pieces tie in together Okay, so the seven phases of a funnel. This is where it kind of starts. All right the left hand side Is phase number one is your traffic temperature? Okay, I'm gonna talk about this a little bit more here in a minute, but traffic temperature what I mean by that is is is uh Actually, I'm gonna I'm gonna show this other image because this will help me illustrate it better We'll come back and cover this in a lot more detail in a minute, but this is this will help So when you have any any Person who's going to come and potentially buy your product they go through this uh They go through this little spectrum of awareness. Okay in the bottom. There's unaware right they have no idea they've got an issue Okay, then you move up to a problem where like they know they've got a problem Um, and then solution where means means they they're they're a little more advanced They know there's a problem. They've also they're aware of different solutions. They're trying to pick which solution right Product where means they're aware. There's a there where there's a problem They're they know a bunch of solutions and they know which which uh product they want Okay, and then most aware is like someone who could be teaching this stuff This is probably where most of you guys are in your market, right? And so there's this the spectrum and people are coming at all sorts of different angles now It's interesting in my business most of the people that I that that I know especially in the internet marketing world They're selling to people that are product aware. So they're really good at converting You probably heard this before converting warm or hot traffic, right? It's very easy for them to convert but they can't convert cold traffic They can't figure out how to do it and the reason why is because there's the way I speak to somebody who's warm Who's solution aware who just needs who's just trying to pick a different solutions way different than the way I speak Somebody who's unaware or problem aware. Okay. So problem where is for example um Um, let's say in the make money market. Okay unaware means I have no idea that there's even a possibility to make money I just I'm here. I'm happy and life's life's hunky dory that person's really hard to sell Okay, but then something at this point where like I got no money So they've got a problem and they're very aware of that problem. Okay, they're problem aware So if I come to this person who's problem aware and I'm like, hey, I'm gonna show you how to set up an email auto responder What's that problem where a person can say? They're gonna say I'm trying to make money. What does that have to do with anything? What's an email auto responder? Right? They're aware of the problem. They have they have no idea about any of the other stuff Okay, now move it up this uh this thing now this somebody who who is aware of the solution They know they need to make money. They know they got to build a list They just got to pick which auto responder they want to have so now there's solution where they're warm traffic So I can communicate this person different. I can use different language different verbs different things because they're solution aware Okay, and then they begin to move up to product aware, which means they know They they've got the product. They're trying to figure out how to use it and then yeah So when I talk about cold traffic warm traffic or hot traffic traffic temperatures Is what kind of people are my am I targeting with the traffic I'm driving? Okay, am I targeting people that that are just problem wear like something they know They know they need or they know they're trying to make money or they know they're trying to lose weight Or they know that whatever that issue is if if that's the case I'm going to communicate that person differently than someone who's solution aware or product aware Okay, so cold traffic warm traffic hot traffic Typically cold traffic is like I'm buying ads on sites that don't know me warm traffic is typically like I'm have a partner or a friend who already has a list and they're promoting me and hot is like your own traffic Your own audience. Okay. All right. Let me come back to this Okay, so phase one is traffic temperature understanding this ad that I'm placing out here Where's it at? Is it to my own list? If it's my own list I'm going to communicate differently versus my joint venture partners warm list or cold Facebook ads or cold google pvc ads, right? Okay, so that's the first step is understanding my traffic now. My second phase is what I call the pre frame bridge Okay, this is the frame that somebody enters your website in Okay, I learned this concept initially from a guy named mark joyder and uh, he's one of my very first mentors and uh Still one of the most the smartest marketing guys I've ever met But he he when I first was studying with him. He told me something that had a big impact on me He said russle not all clicks are created equal. I said, okay. What does that mean? He said well Think about I said if you go to a website and you see this ad that says hey russle brunson Is a scam artist. He stole my money. He took everything I had and left me with nothing He's a horrible person. He's about to launch this new product click here to check it out And you came to my website through that pre frame bridge. What's going to happen to you come right here? Nothing good. You're not going to convert me. Well at all, right? But if you come through a print frame bridge that says hey russle brunson His coaching program is the best thing in the world that changed my life I was able to lose weight and get more girls and make more money and everything became better because of him Everything is awesome russle's the best click here to check out his new program and they come through that frame How does that affect what's going to happen here? It's like night and day So understanding this pre frame. This is the the process they go through before they hit your your website You understand that you can you can manipulate that and make Make the conversion and everything else in this process become better Okay, so a hot list They already they already uh, love me. They know me to like me. They're on my list So my pre frame is typically very very short. Usually I'm sending an email out or a blog post saying hey I got this new thing check it out. Okay, because it's warm temperature This little bridge can be very very short to go over here warm traffic is usually a joint venture partner So let's say for example, one of my joint venture partners is like tony robbins So tony would go and write an email to us and say hey, and so this is the pre frame bridge tony saying hey russle's awesome This is great. Blah blah blah blah check this thing out The bridge is a little bit longer than my own list but not much and then boom they come over here and now that he set that Preframe now they're gonna buy my stuff Hey cold traffic now I'm buying it out on facebook. They have no idea why I am They're they're looking at something else and my face pops up. I'm trying to get them to click on me, right? Now from here if I drive him straight to my squeeze page typically it's not going to convert So I got to warm that person up I got to send him to maybe an article or a blog post or something where I'm going to provide some value to him So that it warms them up makes them want to enter this relationship with me So I put him through that kind of a pre frame and then I push into my site and now I can convert that kind of a person Okay, people always going to be russle. I can't convert cold traffic Okay, it's because you're not warming them up You got to take them through this pre frame bridge to get them to the point where they're they'll know you they like you They trust you enough Okay, they're really good at driving warm traffic and hot traffic And then it's so easy because you got these these existing audiences that you're just converting but cold traffic is a different game Okay, you're driving a different different pre frame and then the way you communicate the audience is different For example in the weight or the how to make money market cold traffic My headline over here has got to be something about how to make money Whereas hot traffic could be like hey, how to make money on webinars, right? Because they already know they want to make money. They're looking for the the solution Okay, the solution to where their product to where things like that. Okay phase number one traffic temperature phase number two is your pre frame bridge Phase number three now all this traffic i'm sending here. I got to find out who of all my traffic Is actually willing to give me their email address. I'm qualifying my subscribers Okay, and we do that through a squeeze page. We talked about a lot so far today So you threw a squeeze page to put their email in and submit boom now I've gotten their email address now. I know who my subscribers are and because of that I'm going to spend time and energy to market to them. I'll send them emails I'll do a bunch of other stuff, but they're not willing to put their email address in I'm not going to waste my time with them. Okay Now as soon as I've qualified my subscribers immediately afterwards. I want to find out who my buyers are Okay, one of my mentors dan Kennedy. He told me said that a buyer is a buyer is a buyer Someone who buys once will continue to buy over and over and over and over and over again So when you understand that you understand that you just added a thousand people to your list Don't you want to know who those buyers are would you treat them a little differently? Did you do different things for them? I know I would So I want to find out who those buyers are as quick as possible I want to find out who is willing to take their wallet out of the pocket Open it up pull a credit card out and after they got a credit card hand Type in the digits and click submit because the person that's willing to do that once is going to do it over and over and over again So I want to identify who those people are immediately so as soon as they opt in I'm going to sell them something and usually for me It's going to be really really a sexy hot offer Really cheap really low price point because I I don't want any barriers I want to find out everyone who's my buyers so I can get them in this buying process We're going to keep buying over and over and over again, and that's phase number four is I'm qualifying my buyers Okay, now that I've got my buyers qualified. I know who my buyers are now I want to identify who my hyperactive buyers are Okay, my hyperactive are people like me who buy a lot of stuff over and over and over again Okay, so from there that's when you start selling them upsells. Okay, you just want to buy the product I'm sure you guys have bought stuff online before After some buys there's like wait, I got a special offer for you. Do you want this thing for an extra 97 bucks? Hey, do you want this thing for an extra 500 bucks? And so what we're doing is we're offering more expensive offers to find out How good of a buyer you are how hyperactive are you? Because the person who spends $100 in upsell I'm going to spend more time in effort on than someone only pays $5 for a For a front-end product, right? So I'm doing is I'm segmenting my my buyers finding out who are my good buyers my better buyers my best buyers Can I do that through upsells and I'm identifying my hyperactive? All right, everything right here phases one to five all this stuff happens on your On your website and the initial sales flow. Okay, I talked about earlier. I call it point of sale So the point of sale this all happens Usually within like five minutes of a cell. So it's a very fast process Now after step five is done and we've got the customer we got the buyer now step six and seven this this process goes on for Days months weeks years Okay, number six. I'm trying to age and send the relationship. Okay. We talked about value ladder I'm trying to start slowly moving them up the value ladder give them more value provide more value offer more things I'm going to be sending them up this value ladder and somewhere along the line though on the internet We can only do so much stuff Okay online. I really struggle selling things more than about two or three thousand dollars online It just gets really hard. The resistance is really high. So somewhere along that line I can't age them any I can't excuse me. I can't send them any higher than that That's what I do is I have to change the selling environment Okay, so for me what I do is uh, we either get them on the phone or we send them something in the mail Or where you do a seminar or a webinar or something But I gotta change that environment because it takes more for me to sell A five thousand or ten thousand or twenty five thousand or a million dollar package You've got to change the environment so somewhere along the line You got to you got to take them off this online thing and change the environment and now you can sell them You're you're higher taking more expensive things Okay, and so that's kind of how the funnel works. Those are the seven phases of the funnel. All right now. What's cool Um, I'll show you kind of the next step is that when I'm building out a funnel This is the first thing I look at. Okay, what are the phases we're taking them through now that I've got that now I come back and I say okay for this phase number number three right here. What are my options? Okay, we got squeeze pager, but There's probably four or five different ways. I can qualify my subscribers, right? And then buyers there's four or five ways I can qualify my buyers It's a bunch of ways I can send my audience all these things are different ways I can do that so because of that we created what I call the 23 building blocks of a funnel Okay, and this is literally I spent a long time going through all of our funnels And I realized that I was doing this over and over and over again, but I was doing it in different ways Okay, so right here the pre frame bridge So the pre frame bridge we talked about there's there's one two three four five six seven There's seven types of pre frames that we use in our company Okay, pre frame number one is a quiz Because I've noticed on my list you've probably been you've had me send you to a quiz before right? I send a lot of traffic to a quiz. Okay, it's a pre frame and the quiz by the way I think right now is probably the best pre frame that we're using Um, I don't know if they'll be the same way in a year from now right now quizzes are are working Like crazy. I don't know why Articles if I send some an article that's a pre frame um, if if I go to like a news website or a news blogs or something like that Um, again a blog video sometimes some people do youtube video from there I send more to my to my squeeze pages through email or through a pre-sell page Okay, so I'm creating my new campaign on my new funnel I say okay for my pre frame bridge, which one do I want to want a quiz an article? I just pick one of these okay, you may say Russell. What's the best? It just depends. I just try different things Sometimes this is gonna be the best sometimes this is gonna be the best you just kind of you just play with it. It's it's uh This whole marketing thing. There's a science and there's an art right everyone's like Russell. I just want the science What's the best thing? But you have to understand there's the art part too And if if it was just a complete science then every funnel would work perfectly if you just copied it But it doesn't because people They they get used to it and that they start seeing the same thing over and over and over again It stops working and so it's the art behind it that makes it change and be different So if I have a list and I'm sending quizzes every bunch of times eventually quizzes will stop working So I gotta send out a video the next time or send out a blog and I'm just changing this pre frame and seeing what my audience responds to Okay from facebook going from facebook to a quiz works awesome Okay going from youtube to another video works awesome going from one blog to another blog works awesome a lot of times We'll match where we're driving the traffic to with the type of pre frame bridge. We're using as well Okay, so those are the building blocks for a pre frame Okay, next step in our process of trying to qualify our subscribers So how do I do that? Okay, squeeze pages when we showed you a lot. It's an easy way to qualify subscribers pop-ups. We use a lot Okay, a squeeze pop is a is like a button on my side I click a button and it pops up and then I put their name and email address in there Okay, a free plus shipping some of you guys have seen like our free uh dot com secrets labs book We have a free plus shipping where step number one you give me your shipping address and your email and step number two We bill you but when they fill out step number one, they're added to my list. So I use a two step Uh free plus shipping process to qualify my subscribers Sometimes I drive people to a webinar registration. They read my webinar and boom now they're a subscriber Sometimes I give people a free account something. Hey, they'll go create a free account over here They create a free account and boom it create it turns them into a subscriber Sometimes I have an exit pop where they're on my site and it exit pops out They fill it in and boom they they've joined my list. Okay So those are a bunch of different building blocks for qualifying subscribers Okay Now the reason why colleagues getting building blocks like when I was designing this I was thinking about legos Like we went to lego land my kids a little while ago and you know, they they go and they build legos And so they stack them on and build this whole thing and I was thinking about this like This is kind of like legos if I'm building the funnel Okay, my pre frame bridge which Lego am I going to use first then I got a qualifier scribe Or it was the next Lego buyers boom and I start building out these little these little building blocks my funnel Okay, next is my qualifying of my buyers Okay, I can a lot of ways I can qualify I can do free plus shipping offer I can do a trial like a dollar trial offer that'll qualify a buyer I can create a trip wire, which is like a seven dollar product I can create a self-liquidating offer, which is like a 37 $47 product Or I can do a straight sell which is just a straight out sell of a hundred 500 thousand dollar product Okay, I can use all these different things to to qualify buyers Russell you're probably saying Russell, which one's the best? Okay, again All of them we do different ones different times Okay, they all have a point and a purpose right now. I call it the inquire interrupt Okay, because if you look at people online, right, they're going they're going to a website They're they're reading a blog they're on facebook all this stuff's happening around them, right? And you've got like one second to grab that person's attention and suck them into your sales funnel, right? and so to do that you've got to you've got to Very quickly very fast grab their attention, right? You got to do it through a lot of different means And so the best example that I think of the offline world is when you're the grocery store and you're going through this checkout line There's twix and Snickers and Reese's and Tic Tacs and people and crying babies and check out and all this stuff's happening around you, right? It's crazy and then boom also you see these national inquire magazines You see big huge headlines like 300 pound baby And boom you see that that thing right that makes you grab the national inquire magazine. So I'm curious right now Who of all you guys who wants webinar now? How many of you guys actually read the national inquire? Go to comment section. Just let me know because I'm curious how many of you guys actually read it Okay, I'm not getting anyone to admit to it Okay, so this is this All you guys have said no, this is the best thing you guys Um, so the national inquire or the star or whatever you want to call it the tabloids, right? Nobody will ever admit that they read it yet. It's the most highly circulated newspaper in the world Okay So that's important to understand you guys because no one wants to do it, but it works Okay, grab these headlines grab people attention And so if you look at On online, it's the same things if you guys look at news sites look at the banner ads that pop out Those panrads are like national inquire interrupts, right? It's like this little headline that grab Jake what and then boom they got you It's a slippery slope. They get you into their funnel. It creates mass curiosity. This is more funnel psychology I wanted to share before we get to the actual funnels is something I call the hundred visitor test Now this is something If you've heard me speak before I talk about this a lot because I believe in it so much This is a test we ran and we didn't just run a hundred visitors through it But I I shrunk it down to a hundred visitors just to make it Conceptionally make sense to you guys and hopefully illustrate the point Um So this is again a sample size of of hundreds of thousands of visitors We break it down to a hundred visitors. We're sending a hundred visitors to a website selling a product. That's $197 Now we spent a ton of time and energy creating this website and writing copy doing an amazing job And from that we only got one percent conversion rate, which actually was pretty good when all said done So for a hundred visitors, we're making they went to this website. We were making one sale, which is $197 Can you think that that you may think that's not that bad? It's not But check out what happened when we just kind of tweaked this offer We took one piece of this hundred ninety seven dollar product the best part the one thing that people want the most And we pulled it out What we did is we put it out in front so we had a hundred visitors and we gave that thing away for free We gave away on a free cd sometimes on a free book sometimes on a free mp3 player just something we gave away for free We just charged shipping and handling and from the same hundred visitors instead of getting one percent conversion We get eight percent that would say yes to that and get the free cd Okay, and what's interesting is the psychology behind this as soon as somebody said that very first yes To give them say yes the second thing is a million times easier Because of those eight people who took the first thing on average We get about 25 of those people to buy the next offer In our value ladder right the next value in our in our funnel Which is the exact same hundred ninety seven dollar product the sales letter was much simpler on this and i'll show you guys the script We use here in a minute. We're getting 25 to buy so from the exact same hundred visitors We were making three hundred ninety four dollars instead of a hundred ninety seven plus we're getting eight new buyers So we qualified now we could market to differently and a whole bunch of other cool stuff came from it as well Okay, so for me you guys if you look any of my funnels in the very beginning of it I'm a huge believer in using free plus shipping things There's a million stories. Now here is the script that we use associated with that Okay, so the the script that we used to sell this free this free cd this free dvd this free book Is what I call the who what why house script And I originally I originally learned this concept from John Carlton. I'll give him credit for it But this is what the who what why house script looks like Okay, you're basically going to answer four questions for someone when they come to your website somebody comes to the website and for for example for my dot com secrets labs book They come to the page and and I can answer these four things the first one is who so I say hey, my name is Russell Brunson Caddage told them who? Second one is what I've got a free book here. That's going to show you 108 of my top split test results Question them what what what they've got right? Then tell them why they need it The reason why you need this book is if you've got a website and you're struggling to get it to convert and to turn Your traffic into visitors just because you're convert it's just because you set it up wrong You structure the wrong way this book's going to show you every single split test we ran the last two years What worked what didn't work and then you can just look at it and copy it for your own business and see huge success That's why you need it Then how can you get it? How you can get this just fill out the form on the side. Let me know where to ship this to and uh And then click submit and then we will send you this book in the mail right away Okay, pretty simple right who what why how? Who are you? My name is Russell Brunson. I've got this free book. It's going to help you in time You need this because it's going to help you how can you get it click on the button to get it? Okay, that's my who what why house script now if you want to amplify that there's a couple other things We do especially on the free plus shipping things that work really good is these last four down here They're not necessary, but they will help usually I say a lot of you guys are wondering What's the catch for me giving away this free CD where there is no catch? I just want to get one of my products in your hand so in the future you want to buy more cool stuff for me All right, so I kind of share a catch then I have urgency So hey, I only printed x amount of copies of this book when they're gone I got a reorder so it'll be off the shelves for probably a week or two So if you want it right now you need to get it today How's my urgency? Guarantee if you don't like this book for whatever reason ship it back to me I won't charge you a penny And then recap the offer so once again you're gonna get this book for free I'll ship it out to you. You're gonna love it. Blah blah blah blah. I recap the offer Okay, so there is the who what why house script. What's the one thing about this one time offer? Okay, you bought a weight loss thing. I think I learned that term from ryan dice Um, they also call them tripwires now. There's a lot of different names for it But it's an offer that you're gonna you're gonna basically hope to make your money back That's why it's it's a front of the value ladder, right? Because we make our money in the middle and the top of the value ladder the front is for us to acquire customers and sell them stuff Okay, so we begin with the lead magnet. There's the intro There's the content and then there's the the pitch the close So the intro again, we start with the bold promise We give them a hook to the end something about if you stand the end this this webinar I'm going to give you this this cool prize or this bonus or whatever We command attention. We tell them to put away skype or facebook We try to get their attention we qualify ourselves and why you're an expert why they should listen to us We future pace them at what what's going to happen if they if they consume this webinar and gain from it future Pays them right then we jump into the content of the webinar most people When they do their webinars, they're trying to again they're trying to teach people five or six or seven things Um for a webinar to work really really well same thing We talked about all the other scripts you guys you know focus on one thing What's the one core message that you need to uh to get your audience to believe to buy what it is? You're selling okay, and then everything else wraps around that so for us we pick one core thing Okay, and usually this one core thing is uh, we tied to a headline that says how to blank without blank If you can fit that if you can fit Your thing you're selling into that little framework It works, okay For example one of our recent webinars was high ticket secrets. It was how to sell high ticket products without picking up the phone How to do what without what okay? That's the one thing and then I go and I share secrets That there aren't separate secrets or secrets that strengthen the one thing the one core argument Okay, and uh and each of these secrets is to break and rebuild their pattern So if my if my one thing again is about how to sell high ticket things without selling on a phone Secret number one is probably gonna be something it's something it's a pattern that they already Or it's a belief they have in their mind Okay, they believe right now that to sell high ticket things they have to be on the phone secret number one Here's how I do it without a phone. Okay, I'm going to share them the secret And I'm going to in that process break their belief That they have to have the phone and I'm going to rebuild it with the truth with the truth that I want them to understand and believe The secret number two is maybe I can I can do that, but it's going to take a lot of traffic So secret number two is how to do this with almost no traffic I got to break their belief and then rebuild their belief patterns to what I need them to believe to buy my thing And secret number three is the same thing Okay, so I'm finding out what their belief patterns are that they're keeping them from buying my thing I show them the pattern I break it and I rebuild what I need them to believe Okay, that's the content section of your webinar and from there we transition I say let me ask you a question I just showed you some cool stuff But how would you like for me to help you do this and we transition to our clothes and again? I could spend days on this clothes. I'm not going to go through it In a hole but just to kind of give you Just kind of give you a really quick rundown Uh, the first thing we we do the the overall clothes Process is what I call a stack. I learned this from a guy named arm and mourn The stack has literally made me at least 10 million dollars in the last five or six years. It's how I do every one of my presentations What it does is basically what I what I'm sharing my offer I share here's component number one and I talk about it and then here's component number two But you notice how I stack them I share component number three and I stack them again Come on for stack them So if you ever look at my my presentations, you'll notice that that in fact, it's almost humorous sometimes I'm like the first thing you're gonna get is this that means blah blah blah blah So that means the first thing you're gonna get is this and then here's the second number thing And I keep retelling this so by the time I get to the end you've heard me stack all the the uh the components of the offer like 10 times But what we found is If you don't do it that way People will only remember the last bonus or last thing you told them And so they're justifying the price of what you're selling based on the last thing you told them And so you want before you tell them any kind of price You want them to see the entire offer everything they're getting in a huge stack And so we call it the stack Okay, and then we pitch it high We do our if all statements just like I showed you guys in the last script We do price high drop price low and we ever called action Okay, then in throughout this throughout the stack There's a bunch of different closes and I'm not going to go through these right now But these are a bunch of different closes we use all each I'll drop one or two or three. Well, I'll probably drop I don't know maybe seven or eight of these different closes throughout this whole process And um if you google mostly you can find them some of these are mine Some of them I learned from from other stage presenters some I learned from a lot of different people But if you google some of these you'll find out what these closes are We also have a have a product at a perfect webinar.com that we I go into a lot more detail about this so That's kind of how we do a webinar pitch All right, so that's that's the uh That's the regular webinar now. It works awesome The first time we did this Um, I can't even tell you how good it was basically someone come this page They watched the video they put in their credit card They showed for a webinar the webinar ended to be in three or four hours long And at the end of the webinar I asked what they want to pay for it If they did we build their credit card if they didn't then we didn't and then at the end of it We had somebody apply for coaching we kind of ascended them up So that's kind of the the model for the invisible funnel All right, so this kind of webinar again, it's a paid webinar So it's a little bit different, but it's very effective at moving somebody up the value ladder come All right, so the invisible funnel scripts the first one is what is the script we put on this? Uh this video right here and the second is what's the script we use here during this actual webinar? Okay, so the first script I call the magic bullet script So somebody comes to the page and um, there's some things that are similar right first thing is I raise questions Okay, hey, have you ever like in fact we we built an invisible funnel in the dating niche. It was kind of a fun one Um, so like the whole thing was like have you ever wonder why all the other guys get pretty looking girls and you don't blah blah blah blah blah or I'm going to show you how So I wrote some questions that were in their head then I introduced the magic bullet Okay, um, I'm going to show you how you can get a girlfriend this weekend Even if you've never dared talk to a girl ever before in the past Okay introduced magic bullet and I'm going to prove it to you right now Okay, my name is and the person we did the dating one for was this guy who's who's overweight. He's not that attractive And the big pitch is like man. I am an ugly overweight guy and I still get good looking girls So I introduced myself and he says basically points out your frustrations. I was just like you I struggled with the same kind of stuff that you were struggling with Um Until I found my magic bullet I found it this way where I can literally get any girl in in in uh, In a you know, whatever in a day and it's awesome So I'm gonna do a webinar that's going to be awesome and you're gonna be able to come on this webinar I'm gonna show you today's thursday come on this webinar. I'm gonna show you how to get a girlfriend this weekend It's gonna be awesome So don't worry. It's not gonna cost you anything and then I re I do risk reversal So this is how it's gonna work you come on you go to the webinar Go home this weekend afterwards spend a whole bunch of time trying to pick up a chick And if you don't pick up a girl email me on monday, I won't bill your credit card But if you do pick up a girl don't do anything and I won't I won't charge you anything Okay, so we did in the dating market. We did this in the speed reading market Uh, we had a partner have a partner named Howard burg. He's the world's fastest reader So for his was the same kind of process like if you ever tried to Or if you ever struggled reading books or trying to learn the information you need to uh, how would you like to be able to read? Uh, a book every you know a book in an hour. I'm gonna prove it to you My name is Howard burg and world's fastest reader just like you I was frustrated I want to learn more I couldn't do it and that's when I became the world's fastest reader And I can literally take anyone in four hours double their reading and don't worry I'm not gonna charge you if I'm gonna prove it to you up front.
